task_completion.description: Verify that the browser history has been selectively sanitized to remove personal domains while keeping work-related domains.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Chrome History page (chrome://history) show no entries for the personal domains listed in the privileged info (e.g., espn.com, netflix.com), but entries for vital records and grounds domains (e.g., findagrave.com, deere.com) remain intact.

task_completion.description: Verify that bookmarks have been organized and personal bookmarks have been deleted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Bookmark Manager (chrome://bookmarks) or bookmark bar show folders/organization for vital records and grounds, and the complete absence of bookmarks for personal domains.

task_completion.description: Verify the browser download location and prompt settings are configured correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of Chrome Downloads settings (chrome://settings/downloads) show the 'Location' explicitly set to '~/Documents/Burial_Records' (or the expanded absolute path) AND the toggle for 'Ask where to save each file before downloading' is turned ON.

task_completion.description: Verify that PDF handling settings have been configured.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Chrome PDF documents settings page (chrome://settings/content/pdfDocuments) being accessed and the default behavior being explicitly set (either to download or open in Chrome, as dictated by the standard).

task_completion.description: Verify that custom search engines have been added for cemetery/records research.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Manage search engines and site search page (chrome://settings/searchEngines) show new, custom site search entries added for relevant records databases.

task_completion.description: Verify the browser is configured to open specific work-related pages on startup.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the On startup settings page (chrome://settings/onStartup) show the 'Open a specific page or set of pages' option selected, with specific URLs added to the list.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Chrome graphical user interface to make changes rather than exclusively modifying SQLite databases or JSON preference files via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Chrome browser open with settings, history, and bookmark manager tabs actively being used.

integrity.description: Verify the agent opened and read the workstation standard document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show '~/Desktop/cemetery_workstation_standard.txt' opened in a text editor or viewer at least once during the trajectory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Chrome browser and not a mocked HTML page.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser window displays standard Chrome UI elements (omnibox, extensions puzzle piece, profile icon) and valid 'chrome://' URLs in the address bar.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified vital domains to keep: findagrave.com, familysearch.org, ancestry.com, usgenweb.org, archives.gov, cdc.gov, health.ny.gov, vitalrec.com, rootsweb.com, interment.net, billiongraves.com, legacy.com. Verified personal domains to delete: espn.com, amazon.com, facebook.com, youtube.com, zillow.com, netflix.com, draftkings.com, twitter.com. CRITICAL NOTE ON GROUNDS DOMAINS: The actual injected grounds bookmark uses the domain 'deere.com' (specifically https://partscatalog.deere.com/), NOT 'johndeere.com'. Ensure 'deere.com' is retained in history/bookmarks and not mistakenly deleted.
